mentions.note: Rallied again on housing recovery and consumer spending resilience; beneficiary of housing stock turnaround.
Greenland peace dividend benefits home improvement retailer; up $953 or 2.5% on tariff relief.
Eviscerated on rate expectations; needs housing turnover and potential Fed rate cuts to thrive; difficult near-term.

Purchased as hedge to expected rate cuts under new Fed chief; backfired as rates rose instead; 30-year Treasury yield now 5%+ kills rate-sensitive housing plays; demonstrates bond market veto power over equity rallies.
Been a huge bust due to higher interest rates and desktop decline; stock may rally if quarter isn't terrible but outlook weak.
Modest beat, 0.6% SSS below expectations, reiterated guidance; early May good; owned as hedge vs. rate cuts; needs housing recovery; if Fed cuts, regret selling.
3% yield is magical level. Can add to positions. Fed rate-hike scenario shouldn't derail DIY demand in spring selling season.
Preferred housing-sector way to play the theme; historically rallies ahead of Fed rate cuts.
Actual buyers showing up, stock flirting with new highs.
Housing-related retail frozen until interest rates fall; holding in charitable trust for rate-cut catalyst; expect winners if peace/Fed cuts materialize.
Up in rally; sole bright spot in home retail.
Down 8% to 52-week low despite 3% yield; Cramer waiting for lower mortgage rates before adding; regards as one of two most problematic positions in portfolio (with AT&T) due to ICE concerns.
New low list, down 2.4%; Cramer's two worst stocks (HD and Nike); signals housing repair demand collapsing.
Hit 2-year low yesterday but surged 5%+ today on ceasefire/rate-cut hopes. Housing still in worst transaction volume in decades; wait for persistent rate cuts before committing.

Rate-cut template stock since 2007 (present since Elmont, Long Island 1987); owns during rate cuts; provides portfolio balance against software/SaaS exposure; get rate cuts with Home Depot, not Intel.
